fields.narrative_intro: *An unbothered person and a hyperventilating person discuss a $400,000 Bitcoin recovery at an airport gate where the flight has been delayed 6 hours.*
fields.panel1:
[setup]
HYPERVENTILATING CROW: "Claude just cracked a Bitcoin wallet from 2015."
UNBOTHERED GHOST: "Oh neat. How?"
fields.panel2:
[build]
HYPERVENTILATING CROW: "It tried 3.5 trillion password combinations."
UNBOTHERED GHOST: "Hm. Why so many?"
fields.panel3:
[pivot]
HYPERVENTILATING CROW: "The guy changed the password while stoned and forgot it."
UNBOTHERED GHOST: "Eleven years ago."
fields.panel4:
[silent reaction]
[Visual: The hyperventilating crow clutches the gate seat armrests. The unbothered ghost floats perfectly still. The departure board behind them flips to DELAYED: 6 HRS. A single tumbleweed rolls past the duty-free shop.]
fields.panel5:
[punch]
UNBOTHERED GHOST: "So an AI did eleven years of math..."
UNBOTHERED GHOST: "...because of one bad Tuesday."
fields.panel6:
[button]
[Visual: The hyperventilating crow pulls out a laptop. On screen: a half-written email to Anthropic titled 'RE: My 2019 crypto password (I was also a little stoned).']

narrative_summary: The joke escalates from a simple headline to the absurd human truth — 3.5 trillion AI computations exist solely because of one impaired decision, landing on the silent implication that this is not a unique situation.
rationale: Candidate 4 earns its punchline twice — 'one bad Tuesday' reframes the astronomical math as a human-scale failure, and the button gag (the crow's half-written email) adds a specific, earned visual that implies universality without stating it. It's the only entry where the closing beat genuinely escalates beyond the setup rather than restating it.
story_title: Bitcoin trader recovers wallet with help of Claude
place: at an airport gate where the flight has been delayed 6 hours
hat_pair: shower cap covered in sticky notes
